ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "firstname" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "lastname" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed and salted) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., Approved, Declined, Pending) */,
  "username" TEXT /* Unique username for each user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user account */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp for the user's last login */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of consecutive failed login attempts */,
  "account_lockout" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the account is locked out due to multiple failed login attempts */,
  "password_reset_token" TEXT /* Token for password reset */,
  "password_reset_expires" TEXT /* Expiry time for the password reset token */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the roles table to link user to roles */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the roles table */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the permissions table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the users table */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp for the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)
